notes: SHIP v1.14.313 - PHONE<->LAPTOP PARITY (handoff 'HANDOFF-phone-laptop-parity.md'; John 'you pick' on the 3 open calls). Problem: the app ran two layouts (phone vs desktop breakpoints) so phone and laptop rendered differently, and the Command ghost vanished on laptop (the wider desktop hero's obsidian surface covered the z-index:-1 #cc-ghost). Decision (John's, in the handoff): ONE phone-width column on every viewport (Instagram-on-desktop; black side margins on wide screens are intended). My 3 picks (owner delegated 'you pick'): (a) desktop layouts GO; (b) cap = 390px NOT the handoff's 440 - 390 is the app's canonical design/verify width ('mock the phone first - 390 before 1080' is a standing design law), so a 390 phone and a 390-capped laptop are truly pixel-identical (440 would miss the 'identical' goal); (c) shipped now as its own version (not stacked edits) - capping to 390 is a PROVABLE no-op on a <=390 phone (a 390 viewport capped to 390 is inert, and every neutralizer lives inside @media(min-width:640) which never fires on a phone), so it cannot regress the phone verify of the .302->.312 stack; it only changes >=640 viewports. FIX = ONE additive CSS block (fully revertible by deleting it; NO edits to the 6 existing desktop breakpoints): (1) BASE cap `body.rd main.main{width:100%;max-width:390px;margin-left:auto;margin-right:auto}` - main.main is the position:relative host of the position:absolute .page layers, so capping it constrains every page. The `width:100%` is load-bearing: main is a FLEX ITEM, and max-width+auto-margins disables the default cross-axis stretch, so with only absolute children its content-width collapsed to 0 (caught in browser verify pre-commit) - width:100% gives it a definite cross-size that max-width then caps to 390; (2) a trailing `@media(min-width:640){...}` placed AFTER all 6 desktop breakpoints so equal-specificity SOURCE ORDER wins (no !important) - re-asserts the phone values the desktop breakpoints changed: #rd-botnav->390 centered, .rf-grid-wrap->2-col/gap14 (undo the 3-col@640 / 4-col@980 restructure), #work-grid/#ref-grid->390, body.rd .page L/R padding->phone gutter (undo the 768/1024/1400 .page desktop padding incl. the 1400 calc), and #pg-cmd .statusrow/.stats/.trow/.sig-h L/R padding+gap->phone (undo the .278 desktop Command-column gutter-zero). Ghost fixed as a side effect: at phone width #cc-ghost lands in the hero right gutter, not covered. SCOPE NOTE (my call, flagged to John): fixed overlays (#rd-*-sheet, #forge3d-sheet) and the shared .app-header stay FULL-WIDTH - only the content column + bottom nav are capped (the parity + ghost target); the handoff scoped only main+botnav+breakpoints, I did not silently expand to the header - John to call whether the top bar should also cap. This REVERTS the device-verified .278 desktop Command column + the .157 responsive 760/1080 grids + caps the N-11 (.312) hero to 390 - all intended by the parity decision. Gates: brace balance 11831->11841 (+10, all from the additive block), 4 inline <script> blocks vm.Script-compile clean, version triple .312->.313 lockstep, uniform LF, ?legacy=1 byte-identical (every selector is body.rd-scoped; the un-scoped desktop rules for .page/.app-header/.hwm/.fiber-cli-pwr are NOT touched so legacy keeps its desktop layout). Browser-verified localhost at BOTH 390 (phone: identical to pre-.313 - the base cap is inert) and 1440 (laptop: 390 centered column, 2-col grids, ghost visible). DEVICE-VERIFY (John - hard gate, test BOTH): (1) phone AND laptop show the SAME 390 column layout, just centered w/ side margins on laptop; (2) Command ghost visible on BOTH; (3) bottom nav centered under the column on laptop, not stretched; (4) Work/Crash-Cart grids are the 2-col phone grid on laptop, not the wide desktop grid; (5) if your phone shows black side bars (letterbox), your device is wider than 390 - tell me the width and I bump the one number; (6) ?legacy=1 unchanged. This is also the consolidated BATCH-VERIFY gate for the whole .302->.313 stack (phone + laptop). NEXT: N-12 phase stepper (backlog).
